# Serious Python (Julien Danjou)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Starting Your Project](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/01-starting-your-project.md): Initializing, structuring, or scaffolding a new Python project, defining or updating Python package version numbers, configuring project layouts, generating boilerplate code, setting up testing hierarchies, or implementing code style/linting configurations (e.g., Flake8, PEP 8 compliance).
    - [Modules, Libraries, and Frameworks](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/02-modules-libraries-and-frameworks.md): Python module management, dependency installation, system imports, library and framework architecture, and API design. Triggered when writing or refactoring Python code, managing `sys.path`, adding external dependencies via `pip`, building custom file importers, or structuring large applications.
    - [Documentation and Good API Practice](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/03-documentation-and-good-api-practice.md): Creating, maintaining, or refactoring Python documentation, configuring documentation generators (Sphinx), designing Python APIs (Application Programming Interfaces), managing library versions, or deprecating existing Python functions/classes.
    - [Handling Timestamps and Time Zones](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/04-handling-timestamps-and-time-zones.md): Python applications involving date, time, timestamp manipulation, serialization, scheduling, event-tracking, and time zone management. These rules are triggered whenever the AI writes, modifies, or reviews code dealing with the `datetime` module or time-based data.
    - [Distributing Your Software](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/05-distributing-your-software.md): Python project distribution, library packaging, PyPI publication, dynamic feature discovery, application extensibility (plugins/drivers), and executable console script creation.
    - [Unit Testing](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/06-unit-testing.md): Writing, refactoring, or reviewing Python unit tests, configuring continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, setting up virtual test environments, or architecting mock objects and test fixtures for Python codebases.
    - [Methods and Decorators](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/07-methods-and-decorators.md): Python code that involves function or class decorators, method definitions (instance, static, class, or abstract), class inheritance, or object-oriented interface design.
    - [Functional Programming](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/08-functional-programming.md): Writing, refactoring, or optimizing Python code that involves data transformations, iterations, list/dictionary/set creation, filtering, mapping, or any scenario where imperative loops and state mutations can be replaced with functional programming paradigms.
    - [The Abstract Syntax Tree, Hy, and Lisp-like Attributes](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/09-the-abstract-syntax-tree-hy-and-lisp-like-attributes.md): Tasks involving Python Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) manipulation, dynamic code parsing/generation, the creation of custom flake8 plugins/checks, and development using the Hy programming language (Python's Lisp dialect).
    - [Performances and Optimizations](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/10-performances-and-optimizations.md): Writing, refactoring, profiling, or optimizing Python code, specifically involving data structure selection, memory management, zero-copy operations, algorithmic efficiency, and dynamic program analysis (profiling/benchmarking).
    - [Scaling and Architecture](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/11-scaling-and-architecture.md): Python application development involving scalability, concurrency, parallelism, network communication, asynchronous I/O, and architectural design for high-performance systems.
    - [Managing Relational Databases](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/12-managing-relational-databases.md): Python code that interacts with relational databases, implements Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) tools (such as SQLAlchemy or Django ORM), designs SQL schemas, writes database migrations, handles data streaming/pub-sub systems linked to database changes, or structures Model-View-Controller (MVC) data access layers.
    - [Write Less, Code More](.roo/vendor/python/serious-python-julien-danjou/13-write-less-code-more.md): Python development tasks involving cross-version compatibility (Python 2 and Python 3), method dispatching, resource management refactoring, and class definition simplification.
